title: CI-RED-020-A: shared API readiness harness + diagnostic boot witness
intent: CI smoke/server-readiness boot loops ended in a bare 'connection refused'/'server not up', masking the real startup cause and making failures look like flakes. Add a stdlib-only readiness utility (poll health paths, fail fast on uvicorn early-exit, print attempted paths + uvicorn log tail) and route the primary smoke workflows through it. CI/governance plumbing only; no product behavior change, no runtime path or endpoint semantics touched. Does not weaken startup validation and does not set RMOS_STRICT_STARTUP=0.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) scripts/ci/wait_for_api_ready.py (new): stdlib-only readiness probe. Polls comma-separated health paths until one returns HTTP 200; if a uvicorn PID file is supplied and the process exits, fails immediately instead of waiting out the timeout; on failure prints attempted URLs, last status/exception per path, and the uvicorn log tail; reports which path became ready. (2) scripts/ci/test_wait_for_api_ready.py (new): stdlib-only unit tests (real http.server + monkeypatch) covering success, multi-path fallback (404 and connection failure), timeout, dead-PID fail-fast, failure-output-with-log-tail, exit codes, and no-third-party-imports. (3) scripts/smoke/run_api_smoke_posts.sh: capture uvicorn stdout/stderr to uvicorn.log, write a PID file, replace fixed sleep 4 with the readiness utility, add trap-based cleanup on success AND failure; readiness and POST-smoke are now separate. (4) api_tests.yml / core_ci.yml (both Boot API jobs) / api_health_check.yml: replace inline boot loops / sleep 3 with the shared utility, with log capture + PID + uvicorn-log upload on failure; readiness checked before endpoint behavior. (5) api_health_and_smoke.yml: keep make api-smoke-posts (inherits the updated script) + upload uvicorn.log on failure.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: This replaces fixed sleeps and bespoke per-workflow curl loops with one shared, tested utility that turns an opaque 'connection refused' into a named startup witness (uvicorn exit + log tail visible in job logs/artifacts). It is not a cosmetic refactor: the diagnostic + fail-fast behavior is new, and standardizing the boot step across workflows is the point. Startup validation is unchanged and not weakened.

commands_run: python scripts/ci/test_wait_for_api_ready.py -> 9 passed (success, 404 fallback, connection-fail fallback, timeout, dead-PID fail-fast, failure-output+log-tail, no-3rd-party-imports, main exit 0/1)
yaml.safe_load of the 4 touched workflows -> all parse OK
note: full real-app boot witness is on Ubuntu CI (local Windows venv app import exceeds 2 min); utility logic is witnessed locally against a real stdlib http.server

files.intent: New stdlib-only readiness probe with diagnostic witness and uvicorn early-exit detection
files.behavior_change: none - CI tooling only; no product/runtime path

files.intent: Stdlib-only unit tests for the readiness utility (no FastAPI/uvicorn deps)
Readiness-gated smoke: log capture, PID, trap cleanup, utility instead of sleep 4
Boot API via the shared utility (paths /health,/api/health) + log upload on failure
Both Boot API loops -> shared utility + per-job uvicorn-log upload on failure
Readiness gate before the POST smoke (separate readiness vs endpoint behavior) + uvicorn-log upload
Inherit readiness via make api-smoke-posts + upload uvicorn.log on failure
